are a professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") team based in [Rosenheim](/wiki/Rosenheim "Rosenheim"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). The team competes in the [DEL2](/wiki/DEL2 "DEL2"), the second highest level of play in professional German ice hockey. They play their home games at the [ROFA-Stadion](/wiki/Rofa-Stadion "Rofa-Stadion"). [ROFA AG](/w/index.php?title=ROFA_AG&action=edit&redlink=1 "ROFA AG (page does not exist)") is also one of the two main sponsors of the Starbulls. The contract with ROFA is currently dated until 2029.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Ice hockey team in Rosenheim, Oberbayern

| Starbulls Rosenheim | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

| League | [DEL2](/wiki/DEL2 "DEL2") |
| Founded | 1928; 98 years ago (1928) |
| Home arena | [ROFA-Stadion](/wiki/Rofa-Stadion "Rofa-Stadion") (capacity: 4,425) |
| Colours | Green, white, red, black |
| General manager | [Christoph Sandner](/w/index.php?title=Christoph_Sandner&action=edit&redlink=1 "Christoph Sandner (page does not exist)") |

| 1994–2000 | Star Bulls Rosenheim GmbH |
| 2000–present | Starbulls Rosenheim e.V. |

The **Starbulls Rosenheim** are a professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") team based in [Rosenheim](/wiki/Rosenheim "Rosenheim"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). The team competes in the [DEL2](/wiki/DEL2 "DEL2"), the second highest level of play in professional German ice hockey. They play their home games at the [ROFA-Stadion](/wiki/Rofa-Stadion "Rofa-Stadion"). [ROFA AG](/w/index.php?title=ROFA_AG&action=edit&redlink=1 "ROFA AG (page does not exist)") is also one of the two main sponsors of the Starbulls. The contract with ROFA is currently dated until 2029.

The team joined [SB/DJK Rosenheim](/wiki/SB/DJK_Rosenheim "SB/DJK Rosenheim") in 1979, after [EV Rosenheim](/wiki/EV_Rosenheim "EV Rosenheim") had folded, and in 1994 the team adopted their current name. The team played in the [Deutsche Eishockey Liga](/wiki/Deutsche_Eishockey_Liga "Deutsche Eishockey Liga") (DEL) from 1995-2000. In 2000, the Star Bulls Rosenheim sold their DEL licence to the [Iserlohn Roosters](/wiki/Iserlohn_Roosters "Iserlohn Roosters"). The current team Starbulls Rosenheim e.V. was founded on May 23, 2000 and had to restart in the lowest league the Bezirksliga.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Starbulls_Rosenheim&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Achievements")]

* ![1st place, gold medalist(s)](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/Gold_medal_icon.svg/20px-Gold_medal_icon.svg.png) **[Deutscher Eishockey-Pokal](/wiki/Deutscher_Eishockey-Pokal "Deutscher Eishockey-Pokal") (DEB-Pokal)**: 2011[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Honored numbers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Starbulls_Rosenheim&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Honored numbers")]

Above the media wall, under the stadium roof there are banners of the following players, honoring their contributions to the organization. However, their numbers aren't retired, as [Mitch Stephens](/w/index.php?title=Mitch_Stephens&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mitch Stephens (page does not exist)") wore number 27 between 2009 and 2012.[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Denis Shevyrin](/w/index.php?title=Denis_Shevyrin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Denis Shevyrin (page does not exist)") wore number 15 in season 2023/24.

## Season-by-season record

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Starbulls_Rosenheim&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Season-by-season record")]

***Note:** **GP** = Games, **W** = Wins, **OTW** = Overtime Wins, **SOW** = Shoot-out Wins, **L** = Losses, **OTL** – Overtime Losses, **SOL** = Shoot-out Losses, **GF** = Goals For, **GA** = Goals Against*

***Point System:** **Win** = 3 points; **OT/SO Win** = 2 points, **OTL/SOL** = 1point*

Source: Elite Prospects[[5]](#cite_note-5)

| Season | League | GP | W | OTW | SOW | L | OTL | SOL | Points | Finish | GF | GA | Postseason |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2022–23 | [Oberliga Süd](/wiki/Oberliga_(ice_hockey) "Oberliga (ice hockey)") | 48 | 36 | 0 | 1 | 9 | 2 | 0 | 112 | 2nd | 169 | 83 | **Won the final against [Blue Devils Weiden](/wiki/Blue_Devils_Weiden "Blue Devils Weiden") 3:1 (best of five series)** |
| 2023–24 | [DEL2](/wiki/DEL2 "DEL2") | 52 | 17 | 5 | 3 | 20 | 5 | 1 | 73 | 11th | 153 | 174 | Saved in first round of relegation against [Bietigheim Steelers](/wiki/SC_Bietigheim_Steelers "SC Bietigheim Steelers") 4:1 (best of seven series, two matches advantage) |
| 2024–25 | DEL2 | 52 | 23 | 4 | 3 | 15 | 5 | 2 | 90 | 5th | 158 | 132 | Quarter final loss against [Dresdner Eislöwen](/wiki/Dresdner_Eisl%C3%B6wen "Dresdner Eislöwen") 1:4 (best of seven series) |
